title: Galarian Stunfisk Battle Guide
summary: A [Ground]/[Steel] physical wall with the signature trapping move [snap-trap] — traps and drains opponents while setting [stealth-rock].
bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
Galarian Stunfisk is a [Ground]/[Steel] physical wall whose niche is the signature trapping move [snap-trap]. The move locks the target in for 4-5 turns while dealing 1/8 HP per turn — a powerful utility against stall pivots like Mega Sableye or [toxapex]. The typing also grants an immunity to [Electric] and resistances to 8 other types.

## Strengths
[Ground]/[Steel] gives 9 resistances plus one immunity — one of the most defensively rich typings. [snap-trap] denies the opponent the chance to pivot out of bad matchups, making it a hard counter to switch-heavy teams. [stealth-rock] support fits naturally onto a slow physical wall.

## Weaknesses & Answers
Four weaknesses — [Fire], [Water], [Ground], [Fighting] — include extremely common offensive types. Speed 32 forces slow pivot play or [trick-room] usage. The base [mimicry] ability is situational at best.

## Recommended Moveset
[snap-trap] is the signature move. [earthquake] is the reliable [Ground] STAB. [stealth-rock] handles hazard setup. [protect] is standard.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[leftovers] is the default recovery item. [rocky-helmet] punishes contact attackers. [mimicry] or [sand-veil] depending on the team. Tera [Steel] patches the [Fire] weakness; Tera [Ground] stacks STAB.

## Teammates & Synergy
Fits [trick-room] and slow-pivot stall cores. Cover [Fire] and [Water] with [Grass] or [Electric] partners. Pair with other stallers for a grinding endgame.

## Play Patterns
Switch in on [Electric] attackers, set [stealth-rock], then [snap-trap] a stall pivot to deny it from the game. Use [earthquake] as general damage. Play for the long game — Galarian Stunfisk's value is denial, not damage.
